comment: LA County agencies — v1 launch metro. Day 1 (structure) seed: agency identity is populated, tip routing fields are honest nulls pending verification (days 2-3). Update routing_last_verified_at when a route is confirmed live; nullable fields stay null until verified, never guessed.

agencies.name: Los Angeles County Sheriff's Department
agencies.notes: Homicide Bureau owns most county-jurisdiction cold cases. Working assumption: tip_route_kind=crime_stoppers_p3, tip_url=LA Crime Stoppers (lacrimestoppers.org). Pending day-2 verification of P3 affiliate and day-3 confirmation of LASD detective routing.

agencies.name: Los Angeles Police Department
agencies.notes: Robbery-Homicide Division owns most city-jurisdiction cold cases. Working assumption: same LA Crime Stoppers P3 portal as LASD. Pending day-2 verification.

agencies.name: Long Beach Police Department
agencies.notes: Independent of LASD jurisdictionally; routes through Long Beach Crime Stoppers, NOT LA Crime Stoppers, per architecture review. P3 affiliate slug + portal URL pending day-2 verification.

agencies.name: Santa Monica Police Department
agencies.notes: Independent of LASD. May not have a P3 affiliate — likely routes via agency_form (web) or agency_phone. Determine in day-2 research.

agencies.name: Pasadena Police Department
agencies.notes: Has its own anonymous tip system per architecture review. Likely tip_route_kind=agency_form. URL pending day-2 verification.

agencies.name: Beverly Hills Police Department
agencies.notes: Independent. May not have a P3 affiliate. Pending verification.

agencies.name: Los Angeles County District Attorney's Bureau of Investigation
agencies.notes: DA's Bureau of Investigation owns a small subset of cases that would otherwise be LASD's. Different tip pathway than LASD Homicide. Day-3 callback to confirm whether they accept public tips on cold cases at all, or whether everything routes through the original investigating agency.

agencies.name: FBI Los Angeles Field Office
agencies.notes: tips.fbi.gov is the federal tip portal — well-publicized and stable. Phone (1-800-CALL-FBI) deliberately left null pending verification of cold-case-specific routing for the LA field office.
